name: Nua Sawan (Thai Beef Jerky)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Top sirloin or top round
4 tbsp Thai light soy sauce (aew sai)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice beef across the grain into pieces measuring approximately 2 inches by 1/4 inch thick.
2. Roast coriander and cumin seeds in a frying pan over low heat until fragrant, then cool and grind coarsely in a mortar or spice grinder.
3. Combine beef and remaining ingredients; marinate for 1 hour.
4. Place beef pieces on a cake rack coated with Pam and set the rack in a foil-lined cookie sheet large enough to catch any liquid dripping from the beef.
5. Place the cookie sheet in an oven set to its lowest setting until the beef pieces are dry to the touch and no liquid seeps out when pressed (approximately 6-12 hours, depending on the oven).
6. Over medium-low heat, heat oil in a flat-bottomed frying pan and add beef in batches of 5-6 pieces.
7. Fry until crispy on the outside and around the edges, then drain on a cake rack over paper towels.
8. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week. Serve alone or with cooked sticky rice.
NOTE: If using other cuts of beef, lightly tenderize them first with a mallet. Drain oil well; excess may become rancid. Cool completely before storing.
